front: Poem: the speaker dives alone, 'not like Cousteau with his assiduous team,' carrying 'a book of myths in which our names do not appear'
Poem: '(The Floating Poem, Unnumbered)' describes the lover's 'slender fingers' finding a 'rose-wet cave' in a sequence inspired by Michelle Cliff
Poem: dedicated to astronomer Caroline Herschel; speaker says 'I am bombarded yet I stand' facing 'a woman in the shape of a monster'
Author: coined the term 'compulsory heterosexuality' in a 1980 essay about 'lesbian existence' from the collection Blood, Bread, and Poetry
Work: essay borrowing an Ibsen title that compares the author's youthful formalism to 'asbestos gloves' and discusses writing 'Aunt Jennifer's Tigers'
back: When We Dead Awaken: Writing as Re-Vision (Adrienne Rich)
work: When We Dead Awaken: Writing as Re-Vision

front: Poem: shares its title with a John Donne poem; contains 'The grammar turned and attacked me' and decries oppression of 'male-made language'
Poem: title creatures are 'bright topaz denizens of a world of green' who 'go on prancing, proud and unafraid'
Author: essay 'Vesuvius at Home' discusses Emily Dickinson; wrote Ghazals: Homage to Ghalib, criticized by Agha Shahid Ali for not following classic rules
